korean.politics: 구윤철 부총리 겸 재정경제부 장관은 삼성전자 파업 가능성에 대해 '파업은 절대 안 된다'며 노사 양측에 원칙 있는 협상을 촉구했다. 정부는 긴급조정권 발동 여부를 두고 막판 논의를 이어갔다.
korean.economy: 한국은행은 삼성전자 총파업이 18일간 지속될 경우 국내 경제성장률이 최대 0.5%p 하락할 수 있다는 보고서를 청와대에 제출했다. 삼성전자는 국내 수출의 22.8%, 코스피 시총의 26%를 차지해 파업의 파급력이 막대할 것으로 우려됐다.
korean.society: 가자 인도주의 구호 선단 나포에 항의하는 시위가 이탈리아와 그리스에서 대규모로 벌어졌다. 쿠바 정부는 미국의 에너지 봉쇄로 인해 원유가 고갈됐다고 공식 발표했다.
korean.international: 세계보건기구는 콩고민주공화국에서 에볼라 감염자가 500명을 넘고 134명이 사망했다고 밝히며 심각한 우려를 표했다. 러시아와 중국은 양국 간 경제 협력 및 주요 국제 현안을 논의하기 위한 정상회담 일정을 조율했다. 한 중국어 번역 소설이 국제 부커상을 수상하며 주목받았다.
korean.tech: 미국이 에볼라 발생국인 콩고민주공화국, 우간다, 남수단 방문 외국인에 대한 입국 금지 조치를 시행했다. AI 반도체 관련 외국인 매수세가 지속되며 관련주가 상승했다.

english.politics: Deputy Prime Minister and Finance Minister Koo Yun-chul issued a stern warning, stating that a strike at Samsung Electronics was 'absolutely unacceptable,' and urged both sides to pursue principled negotiations. The government continued deliberating on whether to invoke emergency arbitration powers.
english.economy: The Bank of Korea submitted a report to the presidential office warning that an 18-day general strike at Samsung Electronics could shave up to 0.5 percentage points off South Korea's economic growth rate this year. Samsung accounts for 22.8% of Korea's total exports and 26% of the KOSPI's total market capitalization, amplifying concerns about its economic impact.
english.society: Large-scale protests erupted in Italy and Greece in solidarity with Gaza-bound aid activists whose flotilla was raided by Israeli forces. Cuba's government officially announced that the island has run out of oil following a prolonged U.S. energy blockade.
english.international: The World Health Organization raised serious concerns over the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where more than 500 cases and 134 suspected deaths had been recorded. Russia and China coordinated plans for a presidential summit to discuss bilateral economic cooperation and key international issues. A novel translated from Mandarin Chinese became the first such work to win the International Booker Prize.
english.tech: The U.S. imposed an entry ban on foreign nationals who had traveled to the DRC, Uganda, or South Sudan in the past three weeks amid the Ebola emergency. Foreign buying of AI and semiconductor-related Korean equities continued, supporting the broader market.

vocabulary.example: The government continued deliberating on whether to invoke emergency arbitration powers.
An 18-day strike could shave up to 0.5 percentage points off South Korea's economic growth rate.
Samsung's dominance in the economy amplifies concerns about the strike's impact.
Large-scale protests erupted in Italy and Greece in solidarity with Gaza-bound aid activists.
Cuba ran out of oil following a prolonged U.S. energy blockade.
The government continued deliberating on whether to invoke emergency arbitration powers.

sentences.sentence: The Bank of Korea submitted a report to the presidential office warning that an 18-day general strike at Samsung Electronics could shave up to 0.5 percentage points off South Korea's economic growth rate this year.
sentences.point: 'warning that + 절'은 '~할 수 있다고 경고하는' 뜻으로 경제 기사에서 자주 쓰이는 분사 구조. 'shave off'는 수치를 줄인다는 뜻의 유용한 표현.
sentences.similar: The central bank issued a statement warning that rising inflation could shave growth projections by one percentage point.

sentences.sentence: Large-scale protests erupted in Italy and Greece in solidarity with Gaza-bound aid activists whose flotilla was raided by Israeli forces.
sentences.point: 'in solidarity with ~(~와 연대하여)'는 집회·시위 기사의 핵심 표현. 'whose + 관계절'로 앞 명사를 수식하는 패턴.
sentences.similar: Students marched in the capital in solidarity with workers whose contracts had been terminated without notice.

sentences.sentence: A novel translated from Mandarin Chinese became the first such work to win the International Booker Prize.
sentences.point: 'the first + 명사 + to + 동사원형'은 최초·선구적 성취를 표현하는 문화·스포츠 기사 전형 패턴.
sentences.similar: She became the first Korean athlete to win a gold medal in the marathon at the Olympics.

expressions.usage: 어떤 집단이나 대의를 지지하기 위해 행동할 때 사용하는 표현
expressions.example: Thousands marched in solidarity with the striking workers outside Samsung headquarters.

expressions.usage: 성장률·비용·시간 등을 소폭 감소시키는 것을 표현할 때 주로 사용
expressions.example: The government warned that the strike could shave off 0.5 percentage points from annual GDP growth.

expressions.usage: 연료·자금·시간 등의 자원이 완전히 소진됐을 때 사용하는 기본 구동사
expressions.example: Cuba announced that the island had run out of oil due to the ongoing energy blockade.

challenge.korean: 한국은행은 삼성전자 파업이 올해 한국 경제성장률을 최대 0.5%포인트 낮출 수 있다고 경고했다.
challenge.answer: The Bank of Korea warned that the Samsung Electronics strike could shave up to 0.5 percentage points off South Korea's economic growth rate this year.
challenge.explanation: 'warn that + 절'은 '~을 경고하다'는 핵심 동사 구조. 'shave off'는 수치를 줄이는 것을 표현하며, 'up to + 숫자'는 '최대 ~까지'라는 상한선을 나타냄.

sources.title: MBC 뉴스 - 삼성 파업 경제 영향
